Motion To Quash The Subpoena For The Personnel Records Of Kathleen Watson From Macy'S West
Off Calendar Dec-01-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David S. Bradshaw for defendant and opposing party: Saks Incorporated

NO OTHER APPEARANCE STATED. HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
Plaintiff's motion to quash employment records subpoena:
Granted in part. The subpoena is modified to require production of documents that were prepared, issued, or in effect during the period from January 1999 through June 1999 describing the job title, duties / job description of plaintiff and any claims of employment discrimination made by plaintiff against Liberty House. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion For Protective Order & Request For Monetary Sanctions, Compendium Of Federal Case Law
Off Calendar Dec-01-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David S. Barshaw for defendant and moving party: Saks Incorporated

NO OTHER APPEARANCE STATED. HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
Defendant's motion for a protective order regarding Charles Hansen and Jeffrey Martin.
Granted in part. The motion is granted as to Charles Hansen. Plaintiff must conduct other discovery to satisfy the test articulated in Spectra Physics Inc. v. Superior Court. The motion is denied as to Jeffrey Martin. Defendant has not presented any evidence to establish good cause for the issuance of a protective order. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to the location and scheduling of the deposition.

All requests for monetary sanctions are denied.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
